name: firebase
description: Firebase gives you a complete backend in minutes - auth, database, storage, functions, hosting. But the ease of setup hides real complexity. Security rules are your last line of defense, and they're often wrong. Firestore queries are limited, and you learn this after you've designed your data model.  This skill covers Firebase Authentication, Firestore, Realtime Database, Cloud Functions, Cloud Storage, and Firebase Hosting. Key insight: Firebase is optimized for read-heavy, denormalized data. I
capabilities: Generate Firestore security rules that properly protect data
Design NoSQL data models optimized for query patterns
Implement Firebase Authentication with OAuth and custom claims
Write Cloud Functions for backend operations and triggers
Set up Firebase Emulators for local development and testing
Optimize Firestore listeners to prevent unexpected costs
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and weak_crypto patterns, but manual review confirms these are false positives. The backtick syntax on line 57 is markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm flags on lines 3, 15, 39, 45, 60 are triggered by generic words like 'security' and 'design', not actual weak crypto usage. This is a legitimate Firebase development skill with no security issues.
valueStatement: Firebase simplifies backend development but security rules and data modeling require expertise to avoid breaches and cost overruns. This skill provides expert guidance on Firebase best practices.
limitations: Cannot provision Firebase projects or manage billing
Cannot write complete application code without specific requirements
Does not replace official Firebase documentation for API details
May not cover niche Firebase services like Remote Config in depth

useCases.title: Secure a new Firestore database
useCases.description: Generate production-ready security rules for a user-owned data collection with proper read/write permissions

useCases.targetUser: Mobile and web developers working on social platforms
useCases.title: Model social app data
useCases.description: Design Firestore collections and queries for a social feed with posts, comments, and likes

useCases.targetUser: Developers wanting to test Firebase features safely
useCases.title: Set up local development
useCases.description: Configure Firebase Emulators to test Auth, Firestore, and Functions without deploying to production

promptTemplates.title: Basic Security Rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Firestore security rules for a collection called 'users' where users can only read and write their own documents
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating basic read/write permissions for authenticated users

promptTemplates.title: Data Model Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a Firestore data model for a blog with posts, comments, and likes. Show the collection structure and example documents
promptTemplates.scenario: Planning NoSQL schema for a content platform

promptTemplates.title: Cloud Function Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Firebase Cloud Function that triggers when a new user is created in Authentication and creates a default profile document in Firestore

promptTemplates.title: Cost Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this Firestore query pattern and suggest optimizations to reduce read costs: collection('posts').where('author', '==', userId).get()
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing database queries for cost efficiency

bestPractices: Always design data models around query patterns, not relationships
Write security rules before deploying any collection to production
Use Firebase Emulators for all development and CI testing
antiPatterns: Skipping security rules and relying on client-side validation only
Modeling Firestore like a relational database with deep nesting
Attaching listeners to entire collections without pagination or limits

faq.question: What is the difference between Firestore and Realtime Database?
faq.answer: Firestore is newer with better querying and scalability. Realtime Database is faster for simple sync scenarios. Choose Firestore for most new projects.

faq.question: How do I secure Firebase functions?
faq.answer: Use Firebase Authentication to verify callers. Validate all input data. Avoid exposing service account credentials. Deploy with --public flag only when needed.

faq.question: Can I use Firebase with my own backend?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Firebase Admin SDK on your server to bypass security rules for trusted operations while keeping client-side rules for users.

faq.question: How do I prevent runaway costs with Firestore?
faq.answer: Add limits to queries, use compound indexes wisely, set up billing alerts, and monitor the usage dashboard regularly.

faq.question: What are Firebase Emulators?
faq.answer: Local development servers that simulate Firestore, Auth, Cloud Functions, and other services. Use them to test without incurring costs or affecting production.

faq.question: How do I handle user roles in Firebase?
faq.answer: Store roles in Firestore user documents or use custom claims in the ID token. Check roles in both client security rules and Cloud Functions.
